# Code of State Regulations
  * [Current Edition](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/csr)
  * [Archived Editions](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/csrPrevious)
  * [Publication Schedule](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/pubsched)
  * [Timeline Calculator](https://s1.sos.mo.gov/adrules/datecalc/)
  * [Rule Copies to Amend](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/agency)
  * [Search Code of State Regulations](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/search/queryCSR)


This website contains regulations as they currently appear in the _Missouri Code of State Regulations (CSR)_.
## Current _CSR_ revision date: March 31, 2026
  * [Title 1 - Office of Administration](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/1csr/1csr)

### About Denny Hoskins
* * *
Denny Hoskins was elected Missouri's 41st secretary of state in 2024. Previously, Hoskins served in the Missouri Senate and the Missouri House of Representatives.   
Hoskins is a fifth-generation Missourian who grew up in central Missouri and graduated from Fatima High School in Westphalia, Missouri, before joining the Missouri Army National Guard and becoming a Certified Public Accountant.

# Accessibility
At the Missouri Secretary of State's office, we take accessibility very seriously, and thus, have made every reasonable effort to make our web site accessible to all users. The site has been designed to comply with the [Missouri IT Accessibility Standard](https://at.mo.gov/ict-laws-standards/), which is based on the federal [Section 508](https://www.section508.gov/) standard for web sites.

# Summaries of Proposed Rules
The following are summaries of proposed rules that the Missouri Secretary of State’s office has submitted for publication in the Missouri Register. These rules will not go into effect until the public has had an opportunity to submit written comments and to attend a public hearing if one is scheduled.
Any person may offer comments on a proposed rule. The instructions for submitting comments are located after the text of each rule. Please note that the official text of a proposed rule is the version that appears in the [Missouri Register](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/moreg/moreg/2025), not the draft copy on this site. Please refer to the official copy if you wish to submit comments.
Proposed Rules | Summary | Fiscal Note(s) | Small Business Impact Statement | Link to Missouri Register | Division  
---|---|---|---|---|---  
[15 CSR 30-51.170](https://www.sos.mo.gov/default.aspx?PageID=10545#proposed) Dishonest or Unethical Business Practices by Broker-Dealers and Agents | This proposed amendment deletes section (3) in order to be consistent with existing case law. | PUBLIC COST: This proposed amendment will not cost state agencies or political subdivisions more than five hundred dollars ($500) in the aggregate. PRIVATE COST: This proposed amendment will not cost private entities more than five hundred dollars ($500) in the aggregate. | This proposed rule does not have an adverse impact on small businesses. | [Missouri Register](https://www.sos.mo.gov/default.aspx?PageID=10545#proposed) | Securities  
[15 CSR 30-51.172](https://www.sos.mo.gov/default.aspx?PageID=10545#proposed) Dishonest or Unethical Business Practices by Investment Advisers and Investment Adviser Representatives | This proposed amendment deletes section (3) in order to be consistent with existing case law. | PUBLIC COST: This proposed amendment will not cost state agencies or political subdivisions more than five hundred dollars ($500) in the aggregate. PRIVATE COST: This proposed amendment will not cost private entities more than five hundred dollars ($500) in the aggregate. | This proposed rule does not have an adverse impact on small businesses. | [Missouri Register](https://www.sos.mo.gov/default.aspx?PageID=10545#proposed) | Securities  
[15 CSR 30-50.030](https://www.sos.mo.gov/default.aspx?PageID=10545#proposed) Fees | This proposed amendment clarifies that the amount for filing a renewal registration for a Regulation A-Tier 2 offering is $100. | PUBLIC COST: This proposed amendment will not cost state agencies or political subdivisions more than five hundred dollars ($500) in the aggregate. PRIVATE COST: This proposed amendment will not cost private entities more than five hundred dollars ($500) in the aggregate. | This proposed rule does not have an adverse impact on small businesses. | [Missouri Register](https://www.sos.mo.gov/default.aspx?PageID=10545#proposed) | Securities  
[15 CSR 30-54.205](https://www.sos.mo.gov/default.aspx?PageID=10545#proposed) Notice Filing Requirements for Regulation A—Tier 2 Offering | This proposed amendment clarifies the process for filing a renewal for a Regulation A—Tier 2 Offering. | PUBLIC COST: This proposed amendment will not cost state agencies or political subdivisions more than five hundred dollars ($500) in the aggregate. PRIVATE COST: This proposed amendment will not cost private entities more than five hundred dollars ($500) in the aggregate. | This proposed rule does not have an adverse impact on small businesses. | [Missouri Register](https://www.sos.mo.gov/default.aspx?PageID=10545#proposed) | Securities  
***These rules may vary slightly from those on the Missouri Secretary of State’s Administrative Rules website as Administrative Rules may make grammar, spelling, date and citation changes. The rules on the Administrative Rule’s website shall take precedent over the rules posted here.**

# Administrative Rules
* * *
The **Administrative Rules** division publishes administrative rules and regulations that are encapsulated in the [Code of State Regulations](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/csr) or CSR, the [Missouri Register, ](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/moreg/moreg)and any current [Emergency Rules](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/EmergenciesforInternet/emergency). [Learn more about the Administrative Rules division.](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/about)
## Contact Information
* * *
**Office of Secretary of State**   
**Administrative Rules Division**  
  
PO Box 1767   
Jefferson City, MO 65102
[(573) 751-4015 (Voice)  
](tel:573-751-4015)  
[(800) 735-2966 (TDD)  
](tel:800-735-2966)
rules@sos.mo.gov
## Featured Items
* * *
  * [Sign up to receive email notifications or revised or new rulemakings.](https://s1.sos.mo.gov/adrules/notifications/CreateUser.aspx)
  * [Login to change your email notification preferences.](https://s1.sos.mo.gov/adrules/notifications/default.aspx)
  * [If you desire these publications in alternate form because of a disability, please contact the Administrative Rules division of the Office of Secretary of State.](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/contact)
  * [Hearing impaired citizens should contact the Administrative Rules division through Relay Missouri (800) 735-2966.](https://relaymissouri.com/)

# Rules With Early Effective Date
This page contains rules that have an early effective date, which results in the rules becoming effective prior to publication in the monthly update to the _Code of State Regulations_. Once these rules appear in the _Code of State Regulations,_ they will be removed from this page.
On this page you will find the order of rulemaking followed by the rule as it will appear in the update to the _Code of State Regulations_.
At the end of the order of rulemaking, it will be noted when the order will appear in the _Missouri Register_ and when the rule will be published in the _Code of State Regulations_.
_**Currently there are no rules with early effective dates.**_

# Emergency Rules
This page contains emergency rulemakings that have not been published in the  _Missouri Register_. Once an emergency rulemaking has been published in the _Missouri Register,_ it can be found at <https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/moreg/moreg/>.
In the authority section of each emergency rulemaking, the issue of the  _Missouri Register_ that it will be published in is listed. In each issue of the  _Missouri Register,_ there is an index listing all of the emergency rulemakings currently in effect.
[**Table of Emergency Rules Currently in Effect**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/main/EmergenciesforInternet/Emtableforweb.pdf)
**TITLE 12—DEPARTMENT OF REVENUE  
Division 10—Director of Revenue  
Chapter 26—Dealer Licensure  
[12 CSR 10-26.230 Dealer Administrative Fees and System Modernization](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/main/EmergenciesforInternet/12c10-26.230IE.pdf)**

[Code of State Regulations](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/csr):: CSR Documents for Agency Update
# CSR Documents for Agency Update
This page is intended to provide current rules in _Microsoft Word_ format for an agency to download for use in making changes to existing rule text. By having the current rule text available, an agency will no longer need to contact the Secretary of State's Administrative Rule Division to have the current rule text emailed or faxed to them. By downloading the rule text from this site to make changes, there will be fewer errors to correct once the rule is filed.
To utilize this site the agency will need to:
  1. Find the chapter the rule appears in;
  2. Select the rule from that chapter;
  3. Download the text;
  4. Edit the rule text as needed; and
  5. Return the WORD format document in hard copy to SOS and JCAR using the current filing process.


* * *
[Expand All | Collapse All]
**Table of Contents**
  * Title 1 - Office of Administration 
    * Division 10 - Commissioner of Administration 
      * [Chapter 1 - Organization](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/main/agency/1csr/1c10-1.doc "Title 1 - Office of Administration | Division 10 - Commissioner of Administration | Chapter 1 - Organization")
      * [Chapter 2 - Privacy of Computer-Accessible, Confidential Personal Information](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/main/agency/1csr/1c10-2.doc "Title 1 - Office of Administration | Division 10 - Commissioner of Administration | Chapter 2 - Privacy of Computer-Accessible, Confidential Personal Information")
      * [Chapter 3 - Preapproval of Claims and Accounts](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/main/agency/1csr/1c10-3.doc "Title 1 - Office of Administration | Division 10 - Commissioner of Administration | Chapter 3 - Preapproval of Claims and Accounts")

[Code of State Regulations](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/csr) : Archived Editions
# Code of State Regulations
This website contains archived editions of regulations as they previously appeared in the _Missouri Code of State Regulations (CSR)_. The Office of the Secretary of State makes no warranties, either express or implied, including warranties regarding the content of this information.
## Archived Editions of _CSR_
  * [Title 1 - Office of Administration](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/1csrpe)
  * [Title 2 - Department of Agriculture](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/2csrpe)
  * [Title 3 - Department of Conservation](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/3csrpe)
  * [Title 4 - Department of Economic Development](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/4csrpe)

# Code of State Regulations
* * *
##  Title 18 - Public Defender Commission
| [Division 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/18csr/18csr#18-10) |
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section" **button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/18csrpe).
* * *
###  division 10 - office of state public defender
  * [Chapter 1](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/18csr/18c10-1.pdf) - Organization of Agency
  * [Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/18csr/18c10-2.pdf) - Definition of Eligible Cases
  * [Chapter 3](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/18csr/18c10-3.pdf) - Guidelines for the Determination of Indigence
  * [Chapter 4](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/18csr/18c10-4.pdf) - Payment of Private Counsel Litigation Costs
  * [Chapter 5](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/18csr/18c10-5.pdf) - Public Defender Fees for Service
  * [Chapter 6](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/18csr/18c10-6.pdf) - Outside Practice of Law by Public Defenders

# Code of State Regulations
* * *
##  Title 14 - Department of Corrections
| [Division 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/14csr/14csr#14-10) | [Division 20](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/14csr/14csr#14-20) | [Division 80](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/14csr/14csr#14-80) |
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section"** button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/14csrpe).
* * *
###  division 10 - director
  * *[Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/14csr/14c10spnotice.pdf) - moved to 7 CSR 10-7
  * [Chapter 5](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/14csr/14c10-5.pdf) - Offender Management


*Moved to Department of Transportation
| [back to top](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/14csr/14csr#top) |
###  division 20 - division of adult institutions
  * [Chapter 1](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/14csr/14c20-1.pdf) - Organization
  * [*Chapter 8](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/14csr/14c20spnotice.pdf) - moved to 14 CSR 10-5
  * [Chapter 28](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/14csr/14c20-28.pdf) - Community Services


*Moved to Division 10

###  division 80 - state board of probation and Parole

# Code of State Regulations
* * *
##  Title 15 - Elected Officials
| [Division 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/15csr/15csr#15-10) | [Division 20](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/15csr/15csr#15-20) | [Division 30](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/15csr/15csr#15-30) | [Division 40](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/15csr/15csr#15-40) | [Division 50](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/15csr/15csr#15-50) | [Division 60](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/15csr/15csr#15-60) |
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section" **button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/15csrpe).

# Code of State Regulations
* * *
##  Title 12 - Department of Revenue
| [Division 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/12csr/12csr#12-10) | [Division 20](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/12csr/12csr#12-20) | [Division 30](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/12csr/12csr#12-30) | [Division 40](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/12csr/12csr#12-40) | [Division 50](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/12csr/12csr#12-50) | [Division 60](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/12csr/12csr#12-60) |
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section"** button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/12csrpe).
ADA compliant Word versions of recently published chapters can be found in the second link marked by chapter number. 
* * *

# Code of State Regulations
* * *
##  Title 16 - Retirement Systems
| [Division 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16csr#16-10) | [Division 20](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16csr#16-20) | [Division 30](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16csr#16-30) | [Division 40](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16csr#16-40) | [Division 50](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16csr#16-50) |
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section"** button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/16csrpe).
* * *
###  division 10 - the public school retirement System of Missouri
  * [Chapter 1](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c10-1.pdf) - Organization and Operation of Board of Trustees
  * [Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c10-2.pdf) - Membership
  * [Chapter 3](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/16csr/16c10-3.pdf) - Funds of Retirement System
  * [Chapter 4](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c10-4.pdf) - Membership and Creditable Service
  * [Chapter 5](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c10-5.pdf) - Retirement, Options and Benefits
  * [Chapter 6](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/16csr/16c10-6.pdf) - The Public Education Employee Retirement System of Missouri


| [back to top](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16csr#top) |
###  division 20 - missouri local government Employees' Retirement System (LAGERS)
  * [Chapter 1](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c20-1.pdf) - General Organization
  * [Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c20-2.pdf) - Administrative Rules
  * [Chapter 3](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c20-3.pdf) - Hearings and Proceedings
  * [Chapter 4](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c20-4.pdf) - Actuarial Assumptions


| [back to top](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16csr#top) |
###  division 30 - missouri state employees' Retirement System
  * [Chapter 1](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c30-1.pdf) - General Organization
  * [Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c30-2.pdf) - Administrative Rules
  * [Chapter 3](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c30-3.pdf) - Missouri State Medical Care Plan


| [back to top](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16csr#top) |
###  division 40 - highways and transportation Employees and Highway Patrol Retirement System
  * [Chapter 1](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c40-1.pdf) - General Organization
  * [Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c40-2.pdf) - General Provisions
  * [Chapter 3](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c40-3.pdf) - Disability Benefits
  * [Chapter 4](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c40-4.pdf) - Survivor Benefits
  * [Chapter 5](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c40-5.pdf) - Assignment of Pension Benefits


| [back to top](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16csr#top) |
###  division 50 - the county employees' retirement Fund
  * [Chapter 1](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c50-1.pdf) - Organization and Operation of Board of Directors
  * [Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c50-2.pdf) - Membership and Benefits
  * [Chapter 3](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c50-3.pdf) - Creditable Service
  * [Chapter 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c50-10.pdf) - County Employees' Defined Contribution Plan
  * [Chapter 20](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/16csr/16c50-20.pdf) - County Employees' Deferred Compensation Plan

# Code of State Regulations
* * *
## Title 17 - Boards of Police Commissioners
| [Division 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/17csr/17csr#17-10) | [ Division 20](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/17csr/17csr#17-20) |
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section" **button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/17csrpe).
* * *
### DIVISION 10 - KANSAS City Board of Police COMMISSIONERS
  * [Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/17csr/17c10-2.pdf "Dvision 10 - Kansas City Board of Police Commissioners, Chapter 2 - Private Security") - Private Security


| [back to top](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/17csr/17csr#top) |
### DIVISION 20 - St. Louis Board of Police Commissioners
  * [Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/17csr/17c20-2.pdf "Division 20 - St. Louis Board of Police Commissioners, Chapter 2 - Private Security Officers") - Private Security Officers
  * [Chapter 3](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/17csr/17c20-3.pdf) - Rules of Couriers
  * [Chapter 4](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/17csr/17c20-4.pdf) - Rules for Private Watchmen
  * [Chapter 5](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/17csr/17c20-5.pdf) - Corporate Security Advisor

# Code of State Regulations
* * *
##  Title 11 - Department of Public Safety
| [Division 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/11csr/11csr#11-10) | [Division 20](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/11csr/11csr#11-20)[Division 30](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/11csr/11csr#11-30) | [Division 40](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/11csr/11csr#11-40) | [Division 45](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/11csr/11csr#11-45) | [Division 50](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/11csr/11csr#11-50) |  
| [Division 60](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/11csr/11csr#11-60) | [Division 70](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/11csr/11csr#11-70) | [Division 75](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/11csr/11csr#11-75) | [Division 80](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/11csr/11csr#11-80) | [Division 85](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/11csr/11csr#11-85) | [Division 90](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/11csr/11csr#11-85)
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section"** button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/11csrpe).

# Code of State Regulations
* * *
##  Title 10 - Department of Natural Resources
| [Division 1](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-1) | [Division 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-10) | [Division 20](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-20) | [Division 22](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-22) | [Division 23](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-23) | [Division 24](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-24) |   
[Division 25](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-25) | [Division 26](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-26) | [Division 30](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-30) | [Division 40](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-40) | [Division 45](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-45) | [Division 50](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-50) |   
[Division 60](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-60) | [Division 70](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-70) | [Division 80](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-80) | [Division 90](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-90) | [Division 100](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-100) | [Division 130](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-130) | [Division 140](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/10csr/10csr#10-140) |
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section"** button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/10csrpe).

###  division 10 - air conservation commission

# Code of State Regulations
* * *
##  Title 1 - Office of Administration
| [Division 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/1csr/1csr#1-10) | [Division 15](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/1csr/1csr#1-15) | [Division 20](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/1csr/1csr#1-20) | [Division 30](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/1csr/1csr#1-30) |  
| [Division 35](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/1csr/1csr#1-35) | [Division 40](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/1csr/1csr#1-40) | [Division 50](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/1csr/1csr#1-50) | [Division 60 |](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/1csr/1csr#1-60) [Division 70 ](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/1csr/1csr#1-70)
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section"** button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**.](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/1csrpe)

  * [Chapter 12](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/1csr/1c10-12.pdf) - State of Missouri-Social Security Manual
  * [Chapter 13](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/1csr/1c10-13.pdf) - Missouri Lottery Payment of Prizes

In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section"** button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/4csrpe).

# Code of State Regulations
* * *
##  Title 6 - Department of Higher Education and Workforce Development
| [Division 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6csr#6-10) | [Division 20](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6csr#6-20) | [Division 25](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6csr#6-25) | [Division 250](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6csr#6-250) | [Division 255](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6csr#6-255) | [Division 300](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6csr#6-300)
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section"** button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/6csrpe).
* * *
###  Division 10 - Commissioner of Higher Education
  * [Chapter 1](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6c10-1.pdf) - Departmental Organization
  * [Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6c10-2.pdf) - Student Financial Assistance Programs
  * [Chapter 3](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6c10-3.pdf) - Residency and Transfer
  * [Chapter 4](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6c10-4.pdf) - Submission of Academic Information, Data and New Programs
  * [Chapter 5](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6c10-5.pdf) - Regulation of Proprietary Schools
  * [Chapter 6](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6c10-6.pdf) - Establishment of New Institutions and Instructional Sites
  * [Chapter 7](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6c10-7.pdf) - Higher Education Contracting
  * [Chapter 8](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6c10-8.pdf) - Dwight D. Eisenhower Mathematics and Science Education Act
  * [Chapter 9](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/6csr/6c10-9.pdf "Division 10 - Commissioner of Higher Education | Chapter 9  - Consumer Information") - Consumer Information
  * [Chapter 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6c10-10.pdf) - Out-of-State Public Institutions
  * *[Chapter 11](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6c10-11.pdf) - Nursing Education Incentive Program (Moved to 20 CSR 2200-7)
  * [Chapter 12](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6c10-12.pdf) - Interstate Reciprocity
  * [Chapter 13](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/AdRules/csr/current/6csr/6c10-13.pdf) - Educational Credit for Military Training or Service
  * [Chapter 14](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/6csr/6c10-14.pdf) - Limit on Tuition Increases


| [back to top](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/6csr/6csr#top) |

* * *
## Title 23 - Missouri Department of the National Guard
[Division 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/23csr#Division%2010)
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a**"Next Section"****"Previous Section"**
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)__CSR_[**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/2csrpe).
ADA compliant Word versions of recently published chapters can be found in the second link marked by chapter number. 
* * *
###  Division 10 - National Guard
  * [Chapter 1](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/23csr/23c10-1.pdf "division 10 - adjutant general | Chapter 1   - General Organization")
  * [Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/23csr/23c10-2.pdf "division 10 - adjutant general | Chapter 2  - Public Access to Training Sites")
  * [Chapter 3](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/23csr/23c10-3.pdf "division 10 - adjutant general | Chapter 3  - National Guard Member Educational Assistance Program") [23c10-3](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/23csr/23c10-3.docx) - National Guard Member Educational Assistance Program
  * [Chapter 4](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/23csr/23c10-4.pdf "division 10 - adjutant general | Chapter 4  - National Guard Military Honor Detail Program")
  * [Chapter 5](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/23csr/23c10-5.pdf "division 10 - adjutant general | Chapter 5  - Missouri Veterans' Recognition Program")
  * [Chapter 6](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/23csr/23c10-6.pdf "division 10 - adjutant general | Chapter 6  - National Guard Armory Rentals")
  * [Chapter 7](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/23csr/23c10-7.pdf "division 10 - adjutant general | Chapter 7  - Missouri Military Family Relief Fund")

# Code of State Regulations
* * *
##  Title 21 - Missouri Family Trust
* * *
| [Division 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/21csr/21csr#21-10) |
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section" **button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/21csrpe).
* * *
###  DIVISION 10 - Director and Board of Trustees
  * [Chapter 1](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/21csr/21c10-1.pdf) - General Organization
  * [Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/21csr/21c10-2.pdf) - Missouri Family Trust
  * [Chapter 3](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/21csr/21c10-3.pdf) - Charitable Trust
  * [Chapter 4](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/21csr/21c10-4.pdf) - Fees

# Code of State Regulations

| [Division 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/5csr/5csr#5-10) | [Division 20](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/5csr/5csr#5-20) | [Division 25](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/5csr/5csr#5-25) | [Division 30](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/5csr/5csr#5-30) | [Division 40](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/5csr/5csr#5-40) | [Division 50](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/5csr/5csr#5-50) |   
| [Division 60](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/5csr/5csr#5-60) | [Division 70](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/5csr/5csr#5-70) | [Division 80](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/5csr/5csr#5-80) | [Division 90](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/5csr/5csr#5-90) | [Division 100](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/5csr/5csr#5-100) | [Division 110](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/5csr/5csr#5-110) |
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section"** button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/5csrpe).
ADA compliant Word versions of recently published chapters can be found in the second link marked by chapter number.

# Code of State Regulations
* * *
## Title 3 - Department of Conservation
* * *
| [Division 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/3csr/3csr#3-10) |
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section"** button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/3csrpe).
* * *
### division 10 - conservation commission
  * [Chapter 1](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/3csr/3c10-1.pdf) - Wildlife Code: Organization
  * [Chapter 2 ](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/3csr/3c10-2.pdf) - Missouri Managed Woods
  * [Chapter 3](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/3csr/3c10-3.pdf) - Wildlife Code: Monetary Values of Fish and Wildlife
  * [Chapter 4](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/3csr/3c10-4.pdf) - Wildlife Code: General Provisions
  * [Chapter 5 ](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/3csr/3c10-5.pdf)- Wildlife Code: Permits
  * [Chapter 6 ](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/3csr/3c10-6.pdf)- Wildlife Code: Sport Fishing: Seasons, Methods, Limits
  * [Chapter 7 ](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/3csr/3c10-7.pdf)- Wildlife Code: Hunting: Seasons, Methods, Limits
  * [Chapter 8](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/3csr/3c10-8.pdf) - Wildlife Code: Trapping: Seasons, Methods
  * [Chapter 9 ](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/3csr/3c10-9.pdf)- Wildlife Code: Confined Wildlife: Privileges, Permits, Standards
  * [Chapter 10 ](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/3csr/3c10-10.pdf)- Wildlife Code: Commercial Permits: Seasons, Methods, Limits
  * [Chapter 11](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/3csr/3c10-11.pdf) - Wildlife Code: Special Regulations for Department Areas
  * [Chapter 12 ](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/3csr/3c10-12.pdf)- Wildlife Code: Special Regulations for Areas Owned by Other Entities
  * [Chapter 20](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/3csr/3c10-20.pdf) - Wildlife Code: Definitions

# Code of State Regulations
* * *
##  Title 2 - Department of Agriculture
| [Division 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2csr#2-10) | [Division 15](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2csr#2-15) | [Division 20](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2csr#2-20) | [Division 30](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2csr#2-30) | [Division 40](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2csr#2-40) | [Division 50](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2csr#2-50) |  
| [Division 60](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2csr#2-60) | [Division 70](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2csr#2-70) | [Division 80](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2csr#2-80) | [Division 90](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2csr#2-90) | [Division 100](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2csr#2-100) | [Division 110](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2csr#2-110) |
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section"** button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/2csrpe).
ADA compliant Word versions of recently published chapters can be found in the second link marked by chapter number. 
* * *
###  division 10 - ag business development
  * [Chapter 1](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2c10-1.pdf) - Organization and Description
  * [Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2c10-2.pdf) - Subscription Fees
  * [Chapter 3](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2c10-3.pdf) - Usage Fees
  * [Chapter 4](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2c10-4.pdf) - AgriMissouri Matching Fund Program
  * [Chapter 5](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2c10-5.pdf)- Price Reporting


| [back to top](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2csr#top) |

| [back to top](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2csr#top) |

  * [Chapter 5](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2c30-5.pdf) - Livestock Markets
  * [Chapter 6](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2c30-6.pdf) - Livestock Markets
  * [Chapter 7](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2c30-7.pdf) - Missouri Livestock Dealer Law
  * [Chapter 8](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2c30-8.pdf) - Poultry
  * [Chapter 9](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2c30-9.pdf) - Animal Care Facilities

  * [Chapter 11](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2c30-11.pdf)- Large Animal Veterinary Student Loan Program
  * [Chapter 21](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2c30-21.pdf) - Requirements for the Missouri Dairy Law

  * [Chapter 36](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2c30-36.pdf) - Egg Quality Program

###  division 60 - grain inspection and Warehousing
  * [Chapter 1](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/2csr/2c60-1.pdf) - Organization and Description

# Code of State Regulations
* * *
##  Title 7 - Missouri Department of Transportation
| [Division 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/7csr/7csr#7-10) | [Division 60](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/7csr/7csr#7-60) | [Division 265](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/7csr/7csr#7-265) |
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section"** button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/7csrpe).
* * *

| [Division 2263](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/20csr/20csr#20-2263) | [Division 2267](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/20csr/20csr#20-2267) | [Division 2270](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/20csr/20csr#20-2270) | [Division 4240 ](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/20csr/20csr#20-4240)|
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section" **button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/20csrpe).
ADA compliant Word versions of recently published chapters can be found in the second link marked by chapter number.

# Code of State Regulations
* * *
## Indexes
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section" **button at the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button at the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
ADA compliant Word versions of recently published chapters can be found in the second link marked by docx. 
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly.

# Code of State Regulations
* * *
##  Title 9 - Department of Mental Health
| [Division 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9csr#9-10) | [Division 20](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9csr#9-20) | [Division 25](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9csr#9-25) | [Division 30](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9csr#9-30) | [Division 40](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9csr#9-40) |  
| [Division 45](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9csr#9-45) | [Division 50](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9csr#9-50) | [Division 60](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9csr#9-60) | [Division 70](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9csr#9-70) | [Division 80](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9csr#9-80) |
In order to minimize download time, some of the larger chapters have been segmented into sections. The segmented chapters will have a **"Next Section"** button in the top and bottom right corners to navigate to the next section and a **"Previous Section"** button in the top and bottom left corners to navigate to the previous section.
The _Code of State Regulations (CSR)_ is updated monthly. Pursuant to section 536.021.8, RSMo, "no rule...shall become effective prior to the thirtieth day after the date of publication of the revision to the Missouri code of state regulations." Therefore, some of the rules published in the current version of the _CSR_ may not be effective. A rule's effective date is found in the authority section at the end of the rule. If a rule's effective date has not arrived yet, the previous edition of the rule would be the rule still in effect. Previous editions of these rules may be available in the [**archived editions**](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/previous/9csrpe).
* * *
###  Division 10 - director, department of mental Health
  * [Chapter 1](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c10-1.pdf) - Organization and Description
  * [Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c10-2.pdf) - Standards for All Residential Facilities Subject to Licensing
  * [Chapter 3](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c10-3.pdf) - Standards for Group Care Homes Subject to Licensing
  * [Chapter 4](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c10-4.pdf) - Standards for Residential Centers Subject to Licensing
  * [Chapter 5](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c10-5.pdf) - General Program Procedures
  * [Chapter 6](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c10-6.pdf) - Human Rights Compliance by Contractors to the Department  

  * [Chapter 7](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c10-7.pdf) - Core Rules for Psychiatric and Substance Use Disorder Treatment Programs
  * [Chapter 31](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c10-31.pdf) - Reimbursement for Services


| [back to top](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9csr#top) |
###  Division 20 - Division of alcohol and drug Abuse
  * [Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c20-2.pdf) - Certification Standards for Alcohol and Drug Abuse Programs


| [back to top](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9csr#top) |
###  Division 25 - fiscal management
  * [Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c25-2.pdf) - Purchase of Service Contracting
  * [Chapter 3](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c25-3.pdf) - Miscellaneous Rules
  * [Chapter 4](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c25-4.pdf) - Vendor Procedures
  * [Chapter 5](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c25-5.pdf) - Administration of Personal Spending Funds
  * [Chapter 9](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c25-9.pdf) - Audit Procedures


| [back to top](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9csr#top) |
###  Division 30 - certification standards
  * [Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c30-2.pdf) - Standards for County-Funded Mental Health Services
  * [Chapter 3](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/9csr/9c30-3.pdf "Division 30 - certification standards | Chapter 3  - Alcohol and Drug Abuse Programs") - Substance Use Disorder Treatment Programs
  * [Chapter 4](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c30-4.pdf) - Mental Health Programs
  * [Chapter 5](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c30-5.pdf) - Standards for Programs for Persons with Mental Retardation  

  * [Chapter 6](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/9csr/9c30-6.pdf "Division 30 - certification standards | Chapter 6  - Certified Community Behavioral Health Organizaion") - Certified Community Behavioral Health Organization
  * [Chapter 7](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/csr/current/9csr/9c30-7.pdf) - Crisis Services


| [back to top](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9csr#top) |
###  Division 40 - licensing rules
  * [Chapter 1](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c40-1.pdf) - Definitions, Licensing Procedures, and General Requirements for Community Residential Programs and Day Programs
  * [Chapter 2](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c40-2.pdf) - Rules for All Day Programs and Community Residential Facilities
  * [Chapter 3](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c40-3.pdf) - Rules for Residential Facilities Licensed by the Division of Aging  
  

  * [Chapter 4](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c40-4.pdf) - Behavioral Health Community Residential Programs
  * [Chapter 5](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c40-5.pdf) - Rules for Group Homes and Residential Centers
  * [Chapter 6](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c40-6.pdf) - Rules for Family Living Arrangements
  * [Chapter 7](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c40-7.pdf) - Rules for Semi-Independent Living Arrangements
  * [Chapter 8](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c40-8.pdf) - Rules for Psychiatric Group Homes
  * [Chapter 9](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c40-9.pdf) - Day Programs Serving Individuals with Mental Illness and Intellectual or Developmental Disabilities
  * [Chapter 10](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c40-10.pdf) - Rules for Day Programs Serving People Who Are Mentally Ill  

  * [Chapter 11](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c40-11.pdf) - Rules for Psychiatric Group Home IIs
  * [Chapter 12](https://www.sos.mo.gov/cmsimages/adrules/csr/current/9csr/9c40-12.pdf) - Rules for Psychiatric Group Home IIIs

MENU
# Administrative Rules FAQs
##  ![expand section](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/adrules/main/images/plus.gif) Filing Rules
  1. What is the easiest way for an agency to obtain the current rule text in Word format for use in preparing amendments to an administrative rule?
Current rule text in Word format is available on the [Secretary of State’s (SOS) Administrative Rule Division website](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules).
  2. Where can I find the fiscal note forms, declaration, and transmittal sheet I need to complete for filing a rule?
Copies of the fiscal note forms, declaration, and transmittal form are available at <https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/forms>. The forms may be downloaded, completed, and saved as a document in your system prior to submission to SOS.
  3. Where do I email an electronic copy of the rule text?
You email an electronic Word copy of the rule to rules@sos.mo.gov prior to filing the original and paper copy(ies) with the Joint Committee on Administrative Rules (JCAR) and Secretary of State (SOS) Administrative Rules Division. 
  4. What is required to be contained in the rulemaking packet filed with Joint Committee on Administrative Rules and the Secretary of State’s office?
The rule packet should contain in this order, 1) a completed transmittal sheet, 2) a certification letter addressed to SOS for the SOS packets, 3) a certification letter addressed to JCAR for the JCAR packet, 4) a public cost declaration, 5) a hard copy of the rule text, 6) a public cost fiscal note if applicable, 7) a private cost fiscal note if applicable, and 8) forms or other included-herein materials if applicable. Put all of these documents in a PDF document for filing. The PDF packet will be emailed to JTCAR@senate.mo.gov _Remember also to email an electronic Word copy of the rule text and fiscal notes, when applicable, prior to filing the rulemaking packets with JCAR and SOS to_rules@sos.mo.gov.
  5. Does the Secretary of State’s office have any format requirements for the rules?
The Secretary of State’s office requires 1) 11- or 12-point type for the rule text, 2) use of only one side of the paper, 3), and a JCAR stamp on the transmittal and on each original and copy of every rule filing. Additionally, amendments are required to have brackets and italics for deleted text and bold type for new text
  6. When is an agency required to file a public fiscal note?
A public fiscal note must be filed if a proposed or emergency rule, amendment, or rescission will require or result in an expenditure of public funds by or a reduction of public revenues for the proposing agency or any other state agency or any political subdivision and which is estimated to cost more than five hundred dollars in the aggregate (over the lifetime of the rule) or, for an emergency, the time the emergency is in effect, to any such agency or political subdivision. The public fiscal note must provide a detailed estimated cost of compliance for each affected agency or each class of affected political subdivision and be supported by a declaration by the director of the department that the estimated cost is reasonably accurate. If the proposed or emergency rule, amendment, or rescission is estimated to cost less than five hundred dollars ($500) in the aggregate or, for an emergency, the time the emergency is in effect, then the agency must file a declaration by the department director to that effect. Fiscal note forms and declarations are available on line at <https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/forms>.
  7. When is an agency required to file a private fiscal note?
A private fiscal note must be filed if a proposed or emergency rule, amendment, or rescission will require an expenditure of money by or reduction in income for any person, firm, corporation, association, partnership, proprietorship, or business entity of any kind or character and is estimated to cost more than five hundred dollars ($500) in the aggregate (over the lifetime of the rule) or, for an emergency, the time the emergency is in effect. The private fiscal note must provide an estimate of the number of persons or business entities by class that would be affected, a classification by types of the business entities in such manner to give reasonable notice of the number and kind of business affected, and an estimate in the aggregate for the cost of compliance for the affected entities. Fiscal note forms are available online at <https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/forms>.
  8. May an agency with cost estimates of more than five hundred dollars ($500) indicate in its public and private fiscal note(s) and declaration that the public or private entity cost is undetermined rather than providing actual cost calculations?
No. The state agency must provide actual cost calculations and projections in the aggregate (over the lifetime of the rule) or, for an emergency, the time the emergency is in effect, for all agencies, political subdivisions, persons, or business entities affected by the rule, or the rule will be void and of no further force and effect. Some agencies do so by providing a first-year cost and then estimated annual costs thereafter for the lifetime of the rule. See, Missouri Hospital Association v. Air Conservation Commission, 874 S.W.2d 380 (Mo. App. W.D. 1994).
  9. What entities are considered political subdivisions for which a state agency must calculate costs in a public cost fiscal note when filing a rule, rule amendment, or rule rescission? 
Section 536.200, RSMo, provides that a political subdivision includes counties, cities, towns, and villages, and school, road, drainage, sewer, water, levee, or any other special-purpose district.
  10. What is incorporated-by-reference material? Where can I find incorporated-by-reference material?
According to section 536.031.4, RSMo, an agency may incorporate by reference rules, regulations, standards, and guidelines of an agency of the United States or a nationally or state recognized organization or association without publishing the material in full. The reference to the material incorporated in the rule must fully identify the incorporated material by publisher, address, and date in order to specify how a copy of the material may be obtained, and shall state that the referenced rule, regulation, standard, or guideline does not include any later amendments or additions.
The agency incorporating a rule, regulation, standard, or guideline must maintain a copy of the referenced rule, regulation, standard, or guideline at the headquarters of the agency and make it available to the public for inspection and copying at no more than the actual cost of reproduction. Materials incorporated by reference must be on permanent file with the agency and may not be discarded when the materials are updated.
  11. What is the difference in meaning between incorporated-by-reference material and included-herein material?
If material is referred to as “included herein,” then the material must actually accompany the rule and be published with it. 
  12. How does the public submit comments to an agency regarding proposed rules, proposed amendment to rules, or the proposed rescission of rules?
Comments are submitted to the agency proposing the rule or rule changes. The text of the proposed rule, amendment, or rescission will provide the name and address of the agency or entity where comments must be filed and the deadline for submitting such comments. 
Comments  _are not_ filed directly with the Secretary of State (SOS) Administrative Rules. 
  13. Once an agency has received all comments to a proposed rulemaking, what is the next step in the rulemaking process?
The agency prepares a final order of rulemaking that includes summaries of all the comments received, the agency response to each comment, and any changes made to the proposed rule as a result of the comments. Similar comments may be consolidated into one comment as part of the final order of rulemaking. 
  14. When is the final order of rulemaking filed with SOS?
The final order of rulemaking must be filed with the Secretary of State no later than ninety (90) days from the date for filing public comment, or within ninety (90) days after hearing if a hearing is held on the proposed rulemaking, or it will become void and the process will have to start over.  _Prior to filing with the SOS, the final order must be filed first with the JCAR for their thirty- (30-) day review period_.
  15. After the final order of rulemaking is filed with the Secretary of State, when does the new rule or rule change(s) become effective?
The new rule or rule change becomes effective thirty (30) days after the final order of rulemaking is published in the  _Code of State Regulations_.


##  ![expand section](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/adrules/main/images/plus.gif) Emergency Rules
  1. What is an emergency rule?
Under section 536.025, RSMo, an emergency rule requires an early effective date because of an immediate danger to the public health, safety, or welfare or to preserve a compelling governmental interest.
  2. How quickly can an emergency rule become effective?
Emergency rules may be effective not less than ten (10) business days after filing or at such later date as may be specified in the rule.
  3. How long can an emergency rule stay in effect?
An emergency rulemaking is effective for one hundred eighty (180) calendar days or thirty (30) legislative days, whichever period is longer, and may be terminated at any time by the agency. All emergency rules must include a statement setting forth the dates during which they will be in effect.
  4. My emergency rule is about to expire. May I file another one?
No. Emergency rules are not renewable.  _If a proposed rule is filed at the same time as an emergency, it is possible to have the proposed rule become effective before or by the time the emergency rule expires_. 
  5. Once an emergency rulemaking is filed with the Secretary of State, will it automatically go into effect?
No. Emergency rules are reviewed by the Secretary of State’s office to determine if they comply with the statutory requirements for publication and adoption. See, Section 536.025, RSMo.
  6. Where can I view emergency rulemakings?  
  
Prior to emergency rulemakings being published in the  _Missouri Register,_ they may be viewed online at <https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/EmergenciesforInternet/emergency>. Once they are published in the _Missouri Register,_ they will be removed from the emergency page.  
  

  7. How does an agency or entity that has filed an emergency rule terminate the emergency rule?  
  
The agency may terminate an emergency rule during the period the rule is in effect by filing an order with the Secretary of State, fixing the date of such termination. The order of termination will be published in the  _Missouri Register_.


##  ![expand section](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/adrules/main/images/plus.gif) Rulemaking Manual and Training
  1. Is the rulemaking manual available on the Internet?
Yes. The [ _Missouri State Rulemaking Manual_](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/manual/manual) is online.
  2. Is there a way for my agency to arrange for training on rulemaking?
Yes. The Administrative Rules Division of the Secretary of State conducts rulemaking training sessions for state agencies. To set up a rulemaking class, call (573) 751-4015.


##  ![expand section](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/adrules/main/images/plus.gif)  _Missouri Register_
  1. What is the  _Missouri Register_?
The  _Missouri Register_ is the magazine that sets forth all state agency rulemakings as they proceed through the rulemaking process. Specifically, the  _Register_ will contain emergency rules, proposed rules, final orders of rulemaking, and in additions. The purpose of the  _Missouri Register_ is to allow citizens access to the rulemaking process and the ability to comment on and recommend changes to proposed rules. Additionally, other state government information such as dissolutions of limited liability companies and limited partnerships are published in the  _Register_.
  2. How often is it published?
The  _Missouri Register_ is published twice a month, around the 1st and 15th of each month.
  3. Where can I find it the _Missouri Registe_ r?
The  _Missouri Register_ is online at <https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/moreg/moreg>.


##  ![expand section](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/adrules/main/images/plus.gif)  _Code of State Regulations_
  1. What is the _Code of State Regulations_?
The _Code of State Regulations_ is the set of all state agencies' rules. The rules in the  _Code_ are organized by state agency. 
  2. How often is it published?
An update to the  _Code of State Regulations_ is published once a month on the last day of the month except in January when it is published on the 29th (30th in a leap year). The rulemakings published in the Code become effective thirty (30) days after they are published unless a later day has been chosen by the agency.
  3. Where can I find the _Code of State Regulations_?
The _Code of State Regulations_ is available online at <https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/csr/csr>.


##  ![expand section](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/adrules/main/images/plus.gif) Dissolutions
  1. Do I need to publish the dissolution of my company in the  _Missouri Register_?
Yes. The Secretary of State is required by sections 347.141 and 359.481, RSMo, to publish dissolutions of limited liability companies and limited partnerships. The content requirements for the one-time publishing of these notices are prescribed by these same sections of statute.
  2. How do I file a dissolution for publication in the  _Missouri Register_?
We request that documents submitted for publication in this section be submitted as an attachment in print-ready 8 ½" x 11" manuscript by email to adrules.dissolutions@sos.mo.gov or by faxing to (573) 751-3032.
  3. What is the charge for publishing a dissolution in the  _Missouri Register_?
There is no charge to publish a dissolution in the  _Missouri Register_.


##  ![expand section](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/adrules/main/images/plus.gif) Sovereign Immunity Limit
  1. Where can I find the Sovereign Immunity Limit?
The Sovereign Immunity Limit is published yearly in the  _Missouri Register,_ usually in December or January. The most current limit published can be found in the Rule Changes Since Update table in any issue of the  _Missouri Register_ under 20 CSR Sovereign Immunity Limit. The last page number listed will be the most current limit published.


##  ![expand section](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/adrules/main/images/plus.gif) Medical Malpractice Limit
  1. Where do I find the Medical Malpractice Limit?
The Medical Malpractice Limit is published yearly in the  _Missouri Register,_ usually in March or April. The most current limit published can be found in the Rule Changes Since Update table in the latest issue of the  _Missouri Register_ under 20 CSR Medical Malpractice. The last page number listed will be the most current limit published.

Kirkpatrick State Information Center, 600 W. Main Street, Jefferson City, Missouri, Room 168.

# Missouri State Archives
The Missouri State Archives research room is open to in-person researchers. For your convenience and to expedite your research, appointments are available. To request an appointment email archives@sos.mo.gov or call (573) 751-3280.   
  
Research requests may also be submitted throughour [online request form](https://s1.sos.mo.gov/Records/Archives/ResearchRequest/) archives@sos.mo.gov. Researchers can temporarily submit up to two requests at a time.
* * *
The **Missouri State Archives** is the official repository for state records of permanent and historical value. Its mission is to foster an appreciation of Missouri history and illuminate contemporary public issues by preserving and making available the state's permanent records to its citizens and their government.
![Missouri State Library](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/Archives/Archives.jpg)

# Style Guide for Rule Writers
This guide addresses style that is specific to administrative rules published in the _Missouri Register_ and _Missouri Code of State Regulations_ and is not intended as a comprehensive grammar and usage reference. The intent of this style guide is to familiarize agency rule drafters with the kinds of stylistic vices that Administrative Rules editors seek to correct in rule text. An agency’s rules may contain exceptions to many of the suggestions in this style guide; however, most new rules can be written to conform with the standards contained herein. 
[Style Guide for Rule Writers](https://www.sos.mo.gov/CMSImages/AdRules/main/StyleGuideforRuleWriters/AgencyStyleGuide.pdf)
See the _[Missouri Rulemaking Manual](https://www.sos.mo.gov/adrules/manual/manual) _for information about the rulemaking process, timelines, and filing requirements.

# Notifications
The notifications system permits users to filter selections of rules and rulemakings based on interest(s). This process allows the selection of pending rulemakings by specific title, division, chapter, and/or rule number. Any time a rulemaking change is published that affects your selection, you will automatically be notified via email.
Sign up to receive email notifications of revised or new rulemakings. New users can   
[**register here**](https://s1.sos.mo.gov/adrules/notifications/CreateUser.aspx) or **[Current Users Login](https://s1.sos.mo.gov/adrules/notifications/default.aspx)**.
